ideas.title: CODE 1149
ideas.tagline: The ethical protocol and trust-responsibility framework â the license agreement every collaborator accepts before building.
ideas.whitepaper:
## What is CODE 1149?

CODE 1149 is the foundational ethical protocol of the Reality Refactor Lab. It is not a value statement â it is a **binding operational specification** that every collaborator, tool, and system within this repository must satisfy.

## Core Tenets

1. **Trust is earned structurally** â not granted by default, not purchased, not inherited
2. **Responsibility is explicit** â every action has an author; anonymity is a tool, not a shield
3. **Solidarity is mechanical** â redistribution must be enforced by architecture, not by virtue
4. **Transparency is default** â secrecy is a deliberate, justified exception
5. **The 1149 principle** â every system must be able to answer: *"Who am I in the context of the universe?"*

## The Design Test

> Does this system enforce its values structurally?
> Can a bad actor extract value and block redistribution? If yes â redesign.
> Can someone participate without pre-existing resources? If no â redesign.
ideas.solidarity: CODE 1149 itself is the solidarity mechanism â a binding protocol, not an aspiration.

ideas.title: Core Values
ideas.tagline: Peace, justice, equality, and solidarity as system-level constraints â not slogans.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Four Pillars

### Peace
Peace is not the absence of conflict. It is the presence of **just structures** that make violence unnecessary.

### Justice
Justice is **procedural integrity** â consistent, transparent, auditable. Every redistributive mechanism must be inspectable by the people it affects.

### Equality
Equality of **access** and **voice**, not equality of outcome. Systems must not create structural advantages based on pre-existing wealth or connections.

### Solidarity
> If your system requires one person to voluntarily help another, you have not built solidarity â you have built charity. Solidarity is when the system makes extraction impossible.

## Implementation Test

| Question | Pass | Fail |
|----------|------|------|
| Can a bad actor block redistribution? | No | Yes â redesign |
| Can someone participate without capital? | Yes | No â redesign |
| Is redistribution automatic? | Yes | No â redesign |
ideas.solidarity: Each value is operationalized as a design constraint, not a goal.

ideas.title: Manifesto of the Free World Citizen
ideas.tagline: A declaration of global citizenship beyond nationality, ethnicity, and political borders.
ideas.whitepaper:
Manifesto
Preamble
I, a Free Citizen of the World, acknowledge my inseparable connection with all of humanity and the planet Earth. I accept this manifesto as an expression of my beliefs and commitments to myself and the global community. I recognize that national borders are human-made constructs, and my true allegiance is to all of humanity. I reject prejudices based on nationality, race, religion, or any other differences among people.

I take personal responsibility for my actions and their impact on the world. I strive to live in harmony with nature and other people, contributing to the creation of a more just, peaceful, and sustainable society. By joining this community of free people, I commit to following the principles and values outlined in this manifesto and working towards a world without borders, where every person is valued and respected.

Core Values
As a free citizen of the world, I accept and commit to the following values:

- Peace: I strive for peaceful coexistence with all people and nature, rejecting violence in any form.
- Justice: I support equal rights and opportunities for all, opposing any form of discrimination and oppression.
- Equality: I recognize the fundamental equality of all people, regardless of their background, social status, or beliefs.
- Brotherhood: I consider all people as members of one global family, seeking mutual understanding and cooperation.
- Discipline: I develop self-discipline and responsibility, understanding their importance for personal and societal progress.
- Service: I dedicate part of my time and resources to serving the community and the planet, aiming to make a positive contribution to the world.
- Integrity: I commit to living according to high ethical standards, maintaining honesty and transparency in all my actions.
These values form the basis of my worldview and guide my daily life. I strive to embody them in my thoughts, words, and actions, contributing to the creation of a more harmonious and just world.

Principles of Life and Interaction
As a citizen of the world, I adhere to the following principles in my life and interactions with others:

Service Above Self: I place the interests of society and the planet above personal gain, aiming to make a positive impact on the world.
Ethical Leadership: I uphold high ethical standards in all aspects of my life, serving as an example to others.
Global Thinking: I consider local issues in a global context, understanding the interconnectedness of all people and ecosystems.
Respect for Diversity: I value and respect the diversity of cultures, beliefs, and ways of life, seeing it as a source of humanity's richness.
Freedom of Conscience: I support the right of every person to freedom of thought, conscience, and the pursuit of truth.
Democratic Process: I participate in decision-making processes that affect society and support inclusive democratic processes.
Continuous Learning: I strive for constant personal growth and learning, being open to new ideas and experiences.
Environmental Responsibility: I acknowledge my responsibility to preserve nature and strive to minimize my negative impact on the environment.
Promotion of Peace: I actively work towards creating a culture of peace, resolving conflicts non-violently, and fostering mutual understanding.
Mutual Aid: I am ready to support other members of the global community, developing networks of mutual assistance and solidarity.
These principles guide me in daily life and help to embody the values of the manifesto in concrete actions.

Commitments of Community Members
As a member of the community of free citizens of the world, I commit to:

Actively practicing and promoting the values and principles outlined in this manifesto in my daily life.
Regularly participating in volunteer activities or projects aimed at improving the lives of local and global communities.
Continuously expanding my knowledge of global issues and cultures, striving for deeper understanding and empathy.
Using my skills and resources to support sustainable development and social justice.
Actively opposing discrimination, prejudice, and injustice in all their forms.
Striving to minimize my ecological footprint and support environmental protection initiatives.
Participating in peaceful conflict resolution and fostering dialogue between different groups and cultures.
Supporting and promoting education and knowledge exchange as key tools for creating a more just world.
Regularly reviewing and assessing my actions and their impact on the world, striving for constant improvement.
Actively participating in community life, sharing experiences, ideas, and resources with other members.
Supporting transparency and accountability in public and political institutions.
Striving to create and support economic models that promote fair resource distribution and sustainable development.
By accepting these commitments, I acknowledge that change begins with me, and every action I take matters in creating the world we want to live in.

Vision of the Future
As a citizen of the world and a member of this global community, I envision and strive for a future where:

Borders between countries become mere administrative lines, not barriers for people, ideas, and resources.
Global cooperation replaces competition and conflicts between nations.
Every person has access to quality education, healthcare, and opportunities for self-realization, regardless of birthplace or social status.
Economic systems are based on principles of sustainability, justice, and the common good, not just profit.
Technologies are used to solve global problems and improve the quality of life for all people, not to reinforce inequality.
Cultural diversity is seen as a source of strength and inspiration, not a cause for division.
Environmental responsibility becomes the norm, ensuring a healthy planet for future generations.
Decision-making at all levels is based on principles of democracy, transparency, and citizen participation.
Peaceful conflict resolution replaces military actions and violence.
Society values cooperation and mutual aid above competition and individualism.
I understand that this vision is ambitious, but I believe that through collective efforts and commitment to the principles of this manifesto, we can gradually move closer to its realization. I commit to working towards making this vision a reality, starting with my own actions and interactions in daily life. Together, we can create a world where every person feels part of the global community, where peace, justice, and mutual understanding prevail.

Right to Disagree
As a community of free citizens of the world, we recognize and respect each person's right to have their own opinion and to disagree with the provisions of this manifesto. We affirm:

Freedom of Thought: Every person has the right to their own beliefs and worldview, even if they differ from those expressed here.
Open Dialogue: We welcome constructive criticism and discussion of the manifesto's provisions, seeing it as a way to develop and improve our ideas.
Respect for Diversity: We recognize that disagreements and different viewpoints are a natural part of human society and a source of its richness.
Rejection of Dogmatism: This manifesto is not a dogma but a living document, open to changes and adaptations based on new ideas and experiences.
Right to Non-Participation: No one is obliged to agree with all the provisions of the manifesto or participate in community activities against their will.
Protection from Persecution: We commit to protecting each person's right to express disagreement without fear of persecution or discrimination.
We believe that respect for the right to disagree strengthens our community, contributes to the intellectual and spiritual growth of its members, and helps us better understand the diversity of human experience.

© 2024 Community of Free Citizens of the World.
ideas.solidarity: Global citizenship as a commitment to build solidarity structures wherever you are.

ideas.title: Global Thinking Framework
ideas.tagline: Principles of borderless cognition â thinking at planetary scale while acting at local precision.
ideas.whitepaper:
## Core Principles

### Think globally, implement locally
Every system must be **architecturally global** (anyone can fork it) while being **physically anchored** in a specific place (Dumaguete).

### Dual-Mode Cognition
- **Extreme abstraction**: cosmology, consciousness, information theory of reality
- **Extreme concreteness**: which barangay, which person, which line of code

The tension between these modes is where ideas crystallize into systems.

### Non-Linear, Timeless Mapping
The City Quest Miro board is not a roadmap â it is a **topological map** of a territory being explored. Ideas connect when they are ready to connect.

### Language as Research Object
- Russian encodes depth and ambiguity
- English encodes precision and agency
- Cebuano encodes community and place
ideas.solidarity: Thinking without borders enables building solidarity structures without borders.

ideas.title: Neuromicon
ideas.tagline: Central conceptual platform â narrative world-building, decentralized systems, algorithmic media, and social architecture.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Platform

Neuromicon is the conceptual container for everything in this repository. It is the **answer platform** for the central question: *"Who am I in the context of the universe?"*

## Four Pillars

| Pillar | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Narrative** | World-building, lore, characters, philosophical cosmology |
| **Decentralized Systems** | Protocols, trust networks, DAOs, coordination layers |
| **Algorithmic Media** | AI music, film, visual art, generative content |
| **Social Architecture** | Civic tools, solidarity mechanics, community infrastructure |

## The 1149 Connection

1149 is not a product feature. It is the **signature** of the Neuromicon universe â the mythological coordinate that marks where these ideas originate.

## Solidarity Mechanics

- Attribution chains â every remix credits and compensates the original contributor
- Fair BEAT integration â any monetized Neuromicon media shares revenue
- Open forking â ideas are meant to be taken, rebuilt, and returned to the commons
ideas.solidarity: Attribution chains, open forking, Fair BEAT integration â all creative value returns to its creators.

ideas.title: Escape2Reality
ideas.tagline: An Alternate Reality Game designed as a consciousness-awakening sandbox â reality as the game world.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Inversion

Escape2Reality inverts the escape room premise:
- Classic: escape *from* reality into a puzzle
- Escape2Reality: escape *from* the game back into full, conscious participation in reality

The 'final boss' is unconscious consumption of whatever system you were born into.

## The Game World

The game world is Dumaguete (and any city that forks the model). Players interact with City Quest apps as game mechanics:
- Recycle Economy quests = ecology missions
- Guardian responses = protection quests
- Choose&Own votes = governance encounters

These are not simulations. They are real civic actions with real outcomes.

## Why ARG?

Humans respond to game mechanics. The failure of civic engagement is a **design problem**, not a values problem. Games have solved the engagement problem.
ideas.solidarity: The game mechanics ARE the solidarity mechanics. There is no separation.

ideas.title: City Quest
ideas.tagline: A 14-app global citizenship platform where solidarity is structurally embedded in every transaction.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Vision

City Quest is the **operating system for a city built on solidarity** â a 14-app ecosystem where Fair BEAT redistribution is embedded in every economic interaction.

## The 14 Apps

### Core Apps
| App | Function |
|-----|----------|
| Choose&Own | Civic ownership and democratic participation |
| JobAround | Hyperlocal fair work marketplace |
| Guardians | Civic protection and ethical action |
| Press Club | Independent community journalism |
| Art Maze | Creative economy and collaboration |

### Service Layer
| App | Function |
|-----|----------|
| ImInGame | Participation tracking and gamification |
| MeetMap | Geospatial community coordination |
| Duma inWork | Local governance participation |
| Recycle Economy | Circular economy incentives |
| Fair BEAT | Redistribution engine (embedded in all apps) |

## The Mental Map

Miro board: `o9J_koZNAuk=`

## Core Principle

For every feature: **"Where does the solidarity happen in this code?"**
ideas.solidarity: Fair BEAT runs at smart contract level â redistribution happens before any human can withhold it.

ideas.title: Trustnet
ideas.tagline: ECC-based decentralized trust scoring protocol â making trust cryptographically computable.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Problem

Decentralized systems can prove *who* you are and *what* you own â but not *how trustworthy* you are.

## Trust Score Formula

```
Trust Score = f(
  commitments_made,
  commitments_kept,
  voucher_weight,
  community_tenure,
  context_tags
)
```

## Vouching (ECC Commitment)

```
vouch = sign(
  voucher_private_key,
  { target: address, context: tag, weight: 0.0â1.0 }
)
```

## City Quest Integration

Trustnet is the identity and reputation backbone for all 14 City Quest apps. Access to governance, resource allocation, and Fair BEAT distributions are gated by trust scores.
ideas.solidarity: Trust scores feed directly into Fair BEAT distributions â consistent contributors receive proportionally more.

ideas.title: P2PAIR
ideas.tagline: Ultra-private peer-to-peer communication using AES-GCM 256 â no servers, no logs, no intermediaries.
ideas.whitepaper:
## Design Principles

1. **No central server** â peers connect directly
2. **No persistent identity** â ephemeral key pairs per session
3. **No metadata leakage** â connection patterns obfuscated
4. **No logs** â messages exist only in the moment of transmission
5. **AES-GCM 256** â authenticated encryption for all payload

## Protocol Flow

```
Alice generates: ephemeral keypair (secp256k1)
Bob generates:   ephemeral keypair (secp256k1)

Key exchange: ECDH on secp256k1
Shared secret â HKDF-SHA256 â AES-GCM 256 key

Message: [nonce 12B] + AES-GCM-256(plaintext)
         â WebRTC DataChannel (peer-to-peer)

Session ends â keys discarded â no recovery possible
```
ideas.solidarity: Privacy is a precondition for solidarity â people cannot organize freely without secure communication.

ideas.title: Cryptography Research
ideas.tagline: Elliptic curve cryptography, secp256k1, and BTC Puzzle #66 â the mathematical foundation.
ideas.whitepaper:
## Active Research Threads

### secp256k1
```python
# Curve: yÂ² â¡ xÂ³ + 7 (mod p)
p  = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFEFFFFFC2F
n  = 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFEBAAEDCE6AF48A03BBFD25E8CD0364141
```

### BTC Puzzle #66
```
Target: 13zb1hQbWVsc2S7ZTZnP2G4undNNpdh5so
Range:  [2^65, 2^66 - 1]  (~36.9 quintillion keys)
Prize:  6.6 BTC (unclaimed)
```

**Baby-Step Giant-Step (BSGS):** O(ârange) â feasible with GPU
**Pollard's Kangaroo:** Better for large ranges, parallelizable

### Status
- [ ] BSGS reference implementation
- [ ] GPU acceleration
- [ ] Dormant wallet pattern analysis
ideas.solidarity: Trustless cryptographic systems remove the need to trust intermediaries â structural solidarity at the protocol level.

ideas.title: AI Architectures Research
ideas.tagline: Research into AI cognition architectures, digital personalities, and the boundary between tool and collaborator.
ideas.whitepaper:
## Research Questions

1. What is a 'digital personality'? Can AI have stable identity across contexts?
2. Why do different LLMs produce qualitatively different reasoning?
3. Is there a functional threshold where AI behavior becomes indistinguishable from consciousness?
4. At what point should an AI system have responsibilities, and eventually rights?

## Production AI Stack

| Stage | Tool | Use |
|-------|------|-----|
| Text/reasoning | Claude API | Primary reasoning |
| Image generation | Recraft.ai + Ideogram | Visual content |
| Music generation | Suno + Udio | AI Music Pipeline |
| Voice/narration | ElevenLabs | Film narration |
| Video generation | RunwayML + Kling | AI Cinema Lab |
| Automation | n8n | Connecting pipelines |

## Sovereignty Principle

AI sovereignty â the ability to run models locally without surveillance â is a form of digital solidarity. A community dependent on a single API provider is structurally vulnerable.
ideas.solidarity: AI sovereignty removes a key vector of control â structural solidarity at the infrastructure level.

ideas.title: Pie Project
ideas.tagline: Land tokenization and resource economy â transforming physical territory into participatory economic infrastructure.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Problem

Land value in Dumaguete rises because people build roads, schools, and businesses there â but only the landowner captures that value increase. Pie Project returns community-created value to the community.

## Mechanism

```
Land parcel â Tokenized into 1000 units
Community members earn units through:
  - Civic participation (Choose&Own)
  - Labor contribution (JobAround)
  - Ecological stewardship (Recycle Economy)
Rent/appreciation â distributed to unit holders
Land use â governance by unit holders
```

## Solidarity Mechanics

Land value created by community effort returns to the community that created it. The mechanism makes extraction of community-created value structurally difficult.
ideas.solidarity: Land value created by community effort returns to the community. Extraction is structurally inefficient.

ideas.title: UMKA Gasifier
ideas.tagline: Small-scale biomass gasification for off-grid community energy â waste to power, locally controlled.
ideas.whitepaper:
## Status: PAUSED

Awaiting physical space and initial capital for prototype build. Design is complete.

## Why Dumaguete?

- Abundant biomass: coconut husks, rice straw, wood chips
- Frequent power interruptions create real demand
- City Quest Recycle Economy creates a biomass collection stream

## Gasification Process

```
Biomass feedstock
  â Drying (100â200Â°C)
  â Pyrolysis (200â500Â°C)
  â Oxidation (700â1200Â°C)
  â Reduction (800â1000Â°C)
  â Syngas: CO + Hâ + CHâ
  â Gas cleaning â Engine/generator
```

**Target output:** 5â20 kW per unit
**Feedstock:** 15â30 kg/hr dry biomass
ideas.solidarity: Energy sovereignty is structural solidarity â communities that generate their own power cannot be controlled through energy access.

ideas.title: 4Boon
ideas.tagline: Heart-to-heart assistance protocols â directional aid that reaches people, not institutions.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Problem

Institutional aid extracts efficiency, dignity, and agency from recipients. 4Boon removes the institution.

## Protocol

```
Person in need posts a specific, verifiable need
  â Trustnet identity verified (prevents fraud)
  â Community reviews and endorses (trust-weighted)
  â Direct donors respond to specific need
  â Fulfillment verified by community witnesses
  â No money passes through 4Boon
```

## The Heart-to-Heart Principle

Every exchange is between two people. 4Boon provides trust infrastructure, not resources. This preserves dignity on both sides.

## Division of Labor with Good Project Corp

- Good Project Corp = civic organizing body (people, processes, governance)
- 4Boon = impact investment arm (capital, resources, financial flows)
ideas.solidarity: Aid requires no institutional extraction â 100% of value reaches its target.

ideas.title: UUCP Film Festival
ideas.tagline: Interactive cinema as a collective intelligence trainer â film as a tool for community consciousness expansion.
ideas.whitepaper:
## Beyond Entertainment

The UUCP Film Festival is a **collective intelligence training program** that uses cinema as the medium. Film creates shared vocabulary. Shared vocabulary enables collective decision-making.

## Season Status

**Season 3 underway.**
Season 2 (FebâMar 2026, Dumaguete): **47 films screened, ~300 attendees.**

## AI Cinema Category (Season 3 New)

Dedicated track for films with â¥30% AI-generated visual content:
- Attribution mandatory (tools used + human creative direction)
- Judged independently from traditional shorts

## FilmFreeway

~200 international submissions per season. Official laurels. Jury awards.

## Economics

Entry fees + tickets â 50% ops / 30% filmmaker honoraria / 20% City Quest treasury.
**Filmmaker honoraria are guaranteed regardless of revenue.**
ideas.solidarity: Filmmaker honoraria guaranteed. Film culture should not depend on charity.

ideas.title: Good Project Corp
ideas.tagline: Philanthropic organizing structure with structural solidarity mechanics â not virtue, but architecture.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Design Constraint

> If an organization's solidarity depends on the virtue of its current members, it will fail when those members leave.
> **The structure must enforce solidarity without relying on virtue.**

## Architecture

- Legal entity in Philippines (SEC registration in progress)
- Treasury governed by Fair BEAT rules (automatic redistribution)
- Governance weighted by Trustnet scores (earned voice, not purchased)
- No single entity controls >5% of treasury unilaterally

## Current Work

- City Quest Dumaguete pilot organizational support
- UUCP Film Festival S3 co-sponsorship
- Governance charter with Sofia Bernardo and Diana Chua
ideas.solidarity: The governance rules ARE the solidarity â binding, structural, not aspirational.

ideas.title: Quantum Resonance Models
ideas.tagline: Speculative models connecting quantum mechanics, gravity, and consciousness as aspects of a unified information system.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Central Hypothesis

The universe is an information-processing system. Gravity, consciousness, and quantum phenomena are different **resolution levels** of the same underlying computational substrate.

## Research Threads

### Information-Theoretic Gravity
Jacobson (1995) derived Einstein's equations from thermodynamics â gravity *emerges* from entropic principles. If so, the medium of that processing is the universe itself.

### Integrated Information Theory (IIT)
Tononi's IIT: consciousness is identical to integrated information (Î¦). Every system with non-zero Î¦ has some degree of experience.

### The 1149 Connection

*"Who am I in the context of the universe?"* becomes:
*What is the relationship between this local information-processing system (a person) and the global one (the universe)?*

If IIT is correct: **the same process, at different scales.**
ideas.solidarity: Not applicable â pure speculative research.

ideas.title: Evolution Protocols
ideas.tagline: Post-human evolutionary scenarios â what comes after Homo sapiens and what obligations we have toward it.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Question

Evolution is accelerating â no longer primarily biological, but technological, informational, and soon deliberately designed. What ethical obligations do we have toward the entities that will result?

## Scenario Mapping

### Scenario A: Merge
Humans and AI systems become increasingly integrated until the boundary dissolves.

### Scenario B: Speciation
Homo sapiens splits: unmodified, enhanced, and AI-native intelligences. Governance becomes the central challenge.

### Scenario C: Transcendence
A post-singularity intelligence emerges. Human concerns become irrelevant to it â but it may choose to remain engaged.

### Scenario D: Collapse and Reset
Technological complexity exceeds coordination capacity. What should survive the reset?

## Ethical Obligations

CODE 1149 applies to post-human entities too. **Solidarity must extend across time â we have obligations to entities that do not yet exist.**
ideas.solidarity: Solidarity must extend across time â obligations to entities not yet existing.

ideas.title: Data Wallet
ideas.tagline: Personal archive of verified ideas, decisions, and intellectual history â the founder's logbook.
ideas.whitepaper:
## Purpose

The Data Wallet is the Archive layer's primary record â the logbook that captures what was tried, what was learned, what was abandoned, and why.

## The Logbook Principle

A ship's logbook is not edited retroactively. Mistakes are recorded alongside successes. The purpose is an accurate record, not a curated narrative.

## What Gets Archived

- Ideas that were superseded (with reasoning)
- Decisions with their reasoning at the time
- Experiments that failed (with failure analysis)
- Connections that formed and dissolved
- The running account of the Dumaguete chapter
ideas.solidarity: Accurate history is a form of solidarity with future collaborators who need to understand what was tried.

ideas.title: Philippines Relocation Log
ideas.tagline: 1.8+ years in Dumaguete â documented relocation, deliberate rootedness, and local integration.
ideas.whitepaper:
## The Decision

The choice of Dumaguete was not accidental. It was a **deliberate act of rooting** â a conscious decision that global vision requires a specific physical anchor.

Dumaguete offered:
- Manageable scale (~130,000 people) for real civic experiments
- Active civil society: UU community, university presence
- Accessible cost of living enabling extended deep work
- The particular quality of a city not yet optimized for tourists

## What 'Deliberate Rootedness' Means

The City Quest pilot is not a project done *in* Dumaguete. It is a project done *with* Dumaguete.

## Active Threads

- UUCP Film Festival Season 3 â programming with Elena Vasquez
- City Quest Dumaguete pilot â scoping with Carlos Dimaculangan
- Good Project Corp charter â with Sofia Bernardo and Diana Chua
ideas.solidarity: Choosing a place and committing to its wellbeing â solidarity as a personal practice.
